Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to figure out which cases are taking longer To do that I will calculate the total duration for each case from the first activity Submit Claim to the last one Close Claim

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Starting with Case 2001 It starts on 2024 04 01 09 00 and ends at 10 30 the same day Thats 1 hour and 30 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 Starts at 09 05 on April 1st ends at 11 00 on April 2nd from April 1st 09 05 to April 2nd 11 00 is 25 hours and 55 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 Starts on April 1st at 09 10 ends on April 3rd at 09 30 Thats 48 hours and 20 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2004 Starts at 09 20 on April 1st ends at 10 45 same day 1 hour 25 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 Starts on April 1st at 09 25 ends on April 4th at 14 30 Thats 77 hours and 5 minutes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: So the cases with significantly longer durations are 2002 2003 and 2005

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: All three long cases 2002 2003 and 2005 have at least one Request Additional Documents step 2003 and 2005 have multiple requests

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: High complexity cases 2003 and 2005 required multiple requests which extended the time 2002 had one request but medium complexity So maybe high complexity leads to more requests thus longer duration

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Region B has cases 2002 2004 2005 2004 was low complexity and quick So maybe region B is not the problem unless it is combined with high complexity

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2003 Region A same as 2001 which was fast so maybe region is not the issue here

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In Case 2002 Adjuster Lisa in Region B Case 2005 also has Adjuster Lisa in Region B

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Maybe Adjuster Lisa is taking longer in Region B for high complexity cases

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But 2002 was medium complexity and took a day while 2005 high took 3 days Not sure if the resource is the main issue here

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For example Adjuster Mike handled 2001 quick and 2003 slow Maybe it is more about the complexity than the resource

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the root causes might be High complexity claims require multiple requests for additional documents leading to delays

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Cases in Region B might have some issues but only when combined with high complexity Maybe in Region B high complexity cases are handled by the same adjuster Lisa who might be overwhelmed or the process has bottlenecks there

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The resources handling high complexity cases like Adjuster Lisa and Mike might be overburdened

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I should also check if there is a pattern in the time between requests For 2003 the first request was at 11 00 on April 1 then again at 17 00 the same day and the next day at 16 00 Similarly 2005 had three requests over three days each time with significant delays

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Perhaps the system allows for multiple requests without a clear path to closure leading to extended durations

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Maybe the manager Bill is slow to approve But without more data on other cases handled by Bill it is hard to say Maybe the approval step is a bottleneck for high complexity cases

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Possible mitigations For high complexity cases streamline the process of requesting and receiving documents Maybe set service targets for how quickly additional documents are provided Assign more resources to handle high complexity cases especially in regions like B Analyze if the same adjusters are handling too many high complexity cases and redistribute the workload Implement a system to track and prioritize high complexity cases to ensure they get timely attention Set service targets for approvals and prioritize these cases
